topic.title: architecture-contracts
topic.shortdesc: System topology, release APIs, payload schemas, examples, templates, identifiers, and role enums.

section.title: Release topology
section.p: Expose the services and data boundaries needed for release coordination.

ul.li: API Gateway accepts release packet writes and forwards them to the release service.
Workflow Engine evaluates approval state and staged rollout policy for each release packet.
Notification Service consumes release and incident events before messaging product, engineering, and support leads.

section.title: Release API contract
section.p: Define write, read, and incident creation endpoints against the release service.

ul.li: POST /v1/releases creates a release packet and returns the packet id.
GET /v1/releases/{releaseId} returns the latest release packet, approval state, and rollout lane.
POST /v1/incidents creates a linked incident record when rollout health degrades.

section.title: Release payload schema
section.p: Pin the canonical structure for a release packet.

ul.li: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.
Release packet schema requires rolloutWindow.start and rolloutWindow.end in RFC3339 format.
Release packet schema restricts rollout lanes to canary, staged, or full.

section.title: Release packet example
section.p: Provide a concrete release packet instance for tests and integrations.

ul.li: Example packet owner is release-manager@atlas.dev.
Example packet risk tier is medium and starts with canary before staged rollout.
Example packet rollout window starts at 2026-04-20T09:00:00Z and ends at 2026-04-20T10:00:00Z.

section.title: Client SDK template
section.p: Show the minimum code needed to create a release packet from a client.

ul.li: Client template sends releaseKey REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT to POST /v1/releases.
Client template includes owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, communicationPlan, rolloutWindow, and lanes.
Client template sends an Authorization bearer token.

section.title: Ops config template
section.p: Pin the environment settings required by the benchmark system.

ul.li: Config template requires RELEASE_EVENT_TOPIC and NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL.
Config template requires ROLLOUT_WINDOW_TIMEZONE set to UTC.
Config template requires PAIR_SYNC_MODE set to agent-primary.

section.codeblock: { "RELEASE_EVENT_TOPIC": "atlas.release.events", "NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL": "https://notify.example.internal/hooks/releases", "ROLLOUT_WINDOW_TIMEZONE": "UTC", "PAIR_SYNC_MODE": "agent-primary", "notes": [ "Config template requires RELEASE_EVENT_TOPIC and NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL.", "Config template requires ROLLOUT_WINDOW_TIMEZONE set to UTC.", "Config template requires PAIR_SYNC_MODE set to agent-primary." ] }

section.title: Release key regex
section.p: Keep release identifiers stable across docs, APIs, and runbooks.

ul.li: Release keys match REL-YYYY-MMDD-SLUG in uppercase.
Release key sample REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT must validate cleanly.
Release key regex prevents ambiguous packet identifiers in docs and APIs.

section.title: Role enum
section.p: Expose the minimum stable actor set used across approvals and notifications.

ul.li: release-manager owns packet quality and approval completeness.
engineering-lead owns rollout safety and rollback execution.
support-lead owns customer-impact communication during release and incident flow.
